ai_summary: Validate superconducting transition temperatures and year of first synthesis for high-temperature cuprate superconductors from condensed matter physics literature.
general_notes: All compounds are cuprate high-temperature superconductors. Tc is the bulk superconducting transition temperature in Kelvin measured at ambient pressure on the optimal-doping stoichiometry. Year of first synthesis refers to the first peer-reviewed report of superconductivity in that compound class. Sources: original discovery papers, Reviews of Modern Physics, and standard solid-state physics references.

columns.name: Compound
columns.notes: Cuprate compound formula with common abbreviation in parentheses. Use the full formula for identification.

columns.name: Critical Temperature Tc (K)
columns.notes: Bulk onset superconducting transition temperature in Kelvin at ambient pressure, optimal doping. Integer or one decimal place. For YBCO: 93 K. For LSCO: 38 K. Report the widely cited/consensus value.

columns.name: Year First Synthesized
columns.notes: Year the first peer-reviewed discovery paper was published reporting superconductivity in this compound class. 4-digit year.
